sanskrit:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः।
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते॥
transliteration: na kartṛtvaṃ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ | na karma-phala-saṃyogaṃ svabhāvas tu pravartate ||
literal_meaning: Neither agency nor actions does the Lord create for the world, nor the union with the fruits of action; it is one’s own nature that acts.
contextual_meaning: The embodied self is not the doer; the sense of authorship is superimposed by the guṇas. Recognizing this dissolves performance anxiety and moral overload.
psychological_state: Shifting from internal to external locus of control; reduction of ego-dystonic guilt.
trigger_scenario: A perfectionist manager blames herself for every team failure; learning that systemic factors (guṇas) drive outcomes lets her set boundaries without self-attack.
anchor_text: You are not the doer; let your nature act and let the results go.
